2606.10796.title: Dep-LLM: Training-Free Depression Diagnosis via Evidence-Guided Structured Multi-factor with Reliable LLM Reasoning
2606.10796.paper_summary: Automatic Depression Detection (ADD) from clinical interviews is a pivotal task in computational mental health, yet it remains challenging due to two critical obstacles: 1) difficulty in modeling complex but sparsely distributed depression clues within lengthy, multi-topic clinical interviews, leading to superficial and unreliable reasoning; 2) scarcity of labeled data due to clinical privacy, together with high cost of training and fine-tuning, limiting the deployment of supervised ADD systems. To jointly address these challenges, we propose Dep-LLM, a training-free framework that mirrors the step-by-step reasoning of clinical psychiatrists and operates entirely on frozen off-the-shelf foundation LLMs. Dep-LLM comprises three stages. First, a Chain-of-Thought (CoT) Depression Multi-factor Analysis module structurally decomposes the long dialogue into five clinically aligned themes and produces evidence-grounded rationales, effectively handling long-context dependencies. Second, we introduce Confidence Analysis and Modulation module that quantifies the epistemic reliability from token-level entropy of each rationale and applies an intra-label and inter-theme modulation that amplifies trustworthy signals while suppressing uncertain ones without extra training. Third, a Collaborative Multi-factor Prediction module dynamically integrates multi-factor signals weighted by confidence into the final diagnosis. Extensive experiments on the DAIC-WOZ and E-DAIC datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and generalizability of Dep-LLM: it surpasses zero-shot baseline on nearly all 21 foundation LLMs across 9 metrics such as accuracy, macro F1 and weighted-average F1, and further outperforms state-of-the-art supervised domain-specific LLMs as well as the latest closed-source commercial LLMs, while requiring no extra training.
2606.10796.paper_summary_zh: 自動抑鬱症檢測（ADD）來自臨床訪談，是計算心理健康中的一項關鍵任務，然而，由於兩個主要障礙，它仍然具有挑戰性：1）在冗長的多主題臨床訪談中，建模複雜但稀疏分佈的抑鬱線索的困難，導致表面和不可靠的推理；2）由於臨床隱私，標記數據稀缺，加上訓練和微調的高成本，限制了監督式ADD系統的部署。為了共同解決這些挑戰，我們提出了Dep-LLM，一個無需訓練的框架，模仿臨床精神科醫生的逐步推理，並完全基於現成的凍結基礎LLM運行。Dep-LLM包括三個階段。首先，鏈式思維（CoT）抑鬱多因素分析模塊結構性地將長對話分解為五個臨床對應的主題，並產生基於證據的推理，有效處理長上下文依賴性。其次，我們引入了信心分析和調整模塊，量化每個推理的語義可靠性，通過每個標記的熵，並應用內標籤和主題間的調整，放大可信信號，同時抑制不確定信號，無需額外訓練。第三，協作多因素預測模塊動態整合按信心加權的多因素信號，形成最終診斷。在DAIC-WOZ和E-DAIC數據集上的廣泛實驗證明了Dep-LLM的有效性和通用性：它在幾乎所有21個基礎LLM上超越了零樣本基線，在準確性、宏觀F1和加權平均F1等9個指標上表現優於最新的監督式特定領域LLM及最新的閉源商業LLM，同時不需要額外的訓練。
2606.10796.author: Yiqing Lyu et.al.
2606.10796.authors: Yiqing Lyu, Xianbing Zhao, Buzhou Tang, Ronghuan Jiang
